codeReview.summary: All three client integration docs (OPENCODE.md, OH-MY-PI.md, FACTORY-DROID.md) exist with comprehensive content covering setup instructions, configuration examples, verification steps, troubleshooting, and architecture diagrams. The health endpoint response examples in all three files include the `mcp` field (verified fixed by commit e3ad0bf from fix-docs-accuracy feature). Configuration examples are internally consistent and follow the expected format for each client.

issues.description: Environment variable table documents `TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT` with default 2, but this only applies to standalone-server mode. When running as OpenCode plugin, the env var is `CURSOR_ACP_TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT` (src/plugin.ts:301) with default 3. Users running in plugin mode may be confused when this env var doesn't work as documented.

sharedStateObservations.description: The distinction between standalone-server mode env vars and plugin mode env vars is not documented in any shared knowledge base. Worker documented TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT but not CURSOR_ACP_TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT. This could be added to .factory/library/environment.md or similar.

summary: Documentation passes review. All three client integration docs (OPENCODE.md, OH-MY-PI.md, FACTORY-DROID.md) exist with correct configurations and comprehensive content. The health endpoint examples now correctly include the `mcp` field (verified fixed by commit e3ad0bf from fix-docs-accuracy). The only remaining issue is a non-blocking documentation gap regarding plugin mode vs standalone mode environment variables in FACTORY-DROID.md.
